original_title: APT37 hackers use new malware to breach air-gapped networks
selection_reasoning: This story showcases the sophisticated methods used to breach supposedly isolated systems - a fundamental security concept. It demonstrates how determined adversaries find ways around even the most stringent security measures, using removable media as bridges between isolated and connected systems.

field_note.title: The Bridge Between Realms
field_note.subtitle: How wandering merchants carry more than goods
field_note.excerpt: Even the most isolated fortresses fall when travelers unknowingly carry cursed trinkets that bridge the gap between the protected and the corrupted realms.
field_note.epigraph: No fortress is truly isolated when merchants carry cursed wares that bridge realms.
body_paragraphs: In the northern kingdoms, there exist certain fortresses built upon principles of absolute isolation - their walls touched by no roads, their towers connected to no message networks, their very existence severed from the corrupted pathways that span the known world. Yet even these bastions have fallen to a most ancient form of treachery: the wandering merchant bearing cursed goods.

The shadow-warriors from the northern reaches have perfected the art of enchanting simple trinkets - amulets and talismans that appear as common trade goods. When carried by unwitting travelers between the isolated fortresses and the connected realms, these cursed items activate, creating invisible bridges where none should exist.

The malicious enchantment works through the very human need for connection. A scribe working in the isolated keep possesses both the cursed trinket and, perhaps, legitimate need to occasionally venture into the connected world. The curse lies dormant until this bridge-moment, then awakens to ferry intelligence between realms that were meant to remain forever separate.

What makes this attack so insidious is its patience and its humanity. Unlike the brute force assaults that announce themselves with fire and thunder, this corruption spreads through the very relationships that make isolation bearable - the trusted messenger, the necessary supply run, the brief moment of connection to the outside world. It reminds us that the most impenetrable walls can be breached not through strength, but through the tender mercies we show to our own humanity.
